mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate signals a healthy labor market, supporting consumer confidence and corporate earnings, which typically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 5% but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate above a low threshold indicates monetary tightening, which typically increases borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, negatively impacting equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% and remains there for at least 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly positive (average return > 0.5%) over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand and investment, signaling economic strength that supports corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1000k units
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and HOUST meet the trigger conditions, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low industrial production, a further decline signals weakening manufacturing and potential recessionary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downwards over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines as specified during a contractionary regime, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) over a 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in GDP growth indicates a strong overall economy, which translates into higher corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such occurrences over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, potentially leading to tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.3% MoM but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.2%) over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job creation, indicated by strong PAYEMS growth, reflects a healthy labor market, which supports consumer spending and overall economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth consistently exceeds 150k over a 3-month average, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and increasing retail sales (RSAFS) signifies robust consumer health and spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth > 0.1% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.2%
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS and RSAFS meet their respective growth conditions, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.05%) over any 2-year period.
